biography_summary: Muhammad Faishal bin Ibrahim Khan Surattee is a Singaporean politician and academic who has served as Senior Minister of State for Home Affairs and Acting Minister-in-charge of Muslim Affairs since 2025. He started as a valuer at IRAS (1993-1996), then pursued an academic career at NUS rising to Associate Professor in Real Estate. First elected as MP in 2006 for Marine Parade GRC, he has been re-elected five times. He previously served as Minister of State for Home Affairs and National Development (2020-2025). He oversees DrugFreeSG and offender rehabilitation programmes. A PAP member, he currently represents Marine Parade-Braddell Heights GRC.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with updated career (IRAS valuer, NUS academic), political stances on Muslim affairs/drug rehabilitation, major achievements, person relationships from Wikipedia and web search
